reviews.review_msg: When students share their opinions, she always disagree. Makes the class very hard to take.Grading  criteria is not clear as well. Does not give good feedback.

This professor was difficult to reach and her expectations were very unclear. She was unprepared for lectures, and did not seem to care about whether her students were succeeding in the course or not. She asked not to be reached via email, but then did not give out her office hours. If you can avoid taking this class by her you should.
Not available to reach, did not respond to emails, gavee little to no feedback on testsworks. Class reading focuses primarily on Nietzsche.
Good person, yet not very good teacher. Most readings are not interesting and lectures are especially boring.

He is a very humble teacher with a relaxed attitude. Explains lectures down to the most minute details and is ready to help whenever you need. The lectures are fun and often small. Readings are crucial but easy.

This prof is easy as hell as long as you write what she wants to hear.  Follow her notes verbatim and its an easy B+ or higher...if not you fail. Marking is erratic  with no explaination and her accent is VERY difficult to comprehend... gets off track and has an annoying attitude.  Definately look for other profs if you want to learn

This teacher has no clue how to effectively teach a class.  While it is clear that she is passionate and knowledgeable about the material, she is boring, unclear, and didn't seem to realize that this was a first year course/not the only course people are taking.  I would never take a class with her again.

Would be a great high school teacher - a bit unserious, disorganized, likes to play tricks with students ("oh I'm gonna give you a quiz"), but otherwise she is friendly, generous marker, encourages class participation. It is very hard to do poorly in her class. She's perhaps too harsh for allowing no breaks & forcing you to do ALL the readings!
Very helpful and interesting teacher. Very funny. Sometimes he could be a hard marker on essays.
Greg was a good Prof, he was helpful if we needed feedback. There was a lot of reading for the course but it was overall enjoyable.
